  • 5 Compute median using approxQuantile
  • 6 Print the results
  • 7 Compute the average salary to use for imputation
  • 8 Fill missing SALARY_NUM with the average
  • 9 Show the first 5 rows with the imputed salary
  • 10 Clean EDUCATION_LEVELS_NAME by removing newline and carriage return characters
  • 11 Show first 5 rows to check
  • 12 Count the number of rows retained
  • 13 Export cleaned DataFrame to CSV

Module 3 Assignment: Big Data Visualization on Scale

Author
Affiliation

Kelly Xiao

Boston University

Published

April 5, 2024

Modified

April 5, 2026

System Information

import platform
import psutil

print("Operating System:", platform.system(), platform.release())
print("Python Version:", platform.python_version())
print("Machine:", platform.machine())
print("Processor:", platform.processor())

mem = psutil.virtual_memory()
print("Total Memory (MB):", round(mem.total / (1024**2), 2))
print("Available Memory (MB):", round(mem.available / (1024**2), 2))
Operating System: Linux 6.17.0-1009-aws
Python Version: 3.12.3
Machine: x86_64
Processor: x86_64
Total Memory (MB): 7818.18
Available Memory (MB): 3907.2

0.1 Start your assignment here

1 1.Load the Dataset

import pandas as pd import plotly.express as px import plotly.io as pio from pyspark.sql import SparkSession import re import numpy as np import plotly.graph_objects as go from pyspark.sql.functions import col, split, explode, regexp_replace, transform, when from pyspark.sql import functions as F from pyspark.sql.functions import col, monotonically_increasing_id

np.random.seed(42)

pio.renderers.default = “notebook”

2 Initialize Spark Session

spark = SparkSession.builder.appName(“LightcastData”).getOrCreate()

3 Load Data

df = spark.read.option(“header”, “true”).option(“inferSchema”, “true”).option(“multiLine”,“true”).option(“escape”, “"”).csv(“./data/lightcast_data.csv”) df.createOrReplaceTempView(“job_postings”)

4 Show Schema and Sample Data

print(“—This is Diagnostic check, No need to print it in the final doc—”)

df.printSchema() # comment this line when rendering the submission df.show(5)

#1.1 Casting salary and experience columns

df = df.withColumn( “SALARY_FROM_CLEAN”, when(col(“SALARY_FROM”).rlike(“1+(\.?[0-9]+)?$”), col(“SALARY_FROM”).cast(“double”)) )

df = df.withColumn( “SALARY_TO_CLEAN”, when(col(“SALARY_TO”).rlike(“2+(\.?[0-9]+)?$”), col(“SALARY_TO”).cast(“double”)) ) df = df.withColumn( “SALARY_NUM”, (col(“SALARY_FROM_CLEAN”) + col(“SALARY_TO_CLEAN”)) / 2 )

df = df.withColumn( “EXPERIENCE_CLEAN”, when(col(“MIN_YEARS_EXPERIENCE”).rlike(“3+$”), col(“MIN_YEARS_EXPERIENCE”).cast(“int”)) )

df.select( “SALARY_FROM”, “SALARY_TO”, “SALARY_NUM”, “MIN_YEARS_EXPERIENCE”, “EXPERIENCE_CLEAN” ).show(10)

#1.2 Computing medians

5 Compute median using approxQuantile

median_from = df.approxQuantile(“SALARY_FROM_CLEAN”, [0.5], 0.01)[0] median_to = df.approxQuantile(“SALARY_TO_CLEAN”, [0.5], 0.01)[0] median_salary = df.approxQuantile(“SALARY_NUM”, [0.5], 0.01)[0]

6 Print the results

print(“Median SALARY_FROM:”, median_from) print(“Median SALARY_TO:”, median_to) print(“Median SALARY_NUM:”, median_salary)

#1.3 Inputing missing salaries

7 Compute the average salary to use for imputation

avg_salary = df.selectExpr(“avg(SALARY_NUM) as avg_salary”).collect()[0][“avg_salary”]

8 Fill missing SALARY_NUM with the average

df = df.withColumn( “SALARY_NUM_IMPUTED”, (when(col(“SALARY_NUM”).isNull(), avg_salary).otherwise(col(“SALARY_NUM”))) )

9 Show the first 5 rows with the imputed salary

df.select( “SALARY_NUM”, “SALARY_NUM_IMPUTED”, “EDUCATION_LEVELS_NAME”, “REMOTE_TYPE_NAME”, “MAX_YEARS_EXPERIENCE”, “LOT_V6_SPECIALIZED_OCCUPATION_NAME” ).show(5, truncate=False)

#1.4 Cleaning Education column

10 Clean EDUCATION_LEVELS_NAME by removing newline and carriage return characters

df = df.withColumn( “EDUCATION_LEVELS_NAME_CLEAN”, regexp_replace(col(“EDUCATION_LEVELS_NAME”), “[”, ““) )

11 Show first 5 rows to check

df.select( “EDUCATION_LEVELS_NAME”, “EDUCATION_LEVELS_NAME_CLEAN” ).show(5, truncate=False)

#1.5 Exporting Cleaned Data

12 Count the number of rows retained

rows_retained = df.count() print(f”Data cleaning complete. Rows retained: {rows_retained}“)

13 Export cleaned DataFrame to CSV

output_path = “./data/lightcast_data_cleaned.csv” # adjust path as needed df.write.option(“header”, True).mode(“overwrite”).csv(output_path)

#2. Salary Distribution by Industry and Employment Type
